title: Workbook — Anointing of the Sick
html:
Student Workbook

For use after the lesson or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. Who was St. Camillus de Lellis before his conversion, and what transformed him?
  2. According to the Letter of James (Jas 5:14-15), what should a Christian do when they are sick?
  3. Why did the Second Vatican Council change the name from «Extreme Unction» to «Anointing of the Sick»?
  4. Name at least three effects of this sacrament according to the Catechism (CCC 1520-1523).
  5. What is Viaticum and what does it mean?

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «correct» answers to these personal questions.

1. What image did you have of this sacrament before the lesson? Has anything changed?

Many people associate the Anointing with «the last thing» before dying. If you grew up with that idea, explain it. How do you feel now knowing it is a sacrament for the sick, not just for the dying?

2. Do you know someone who is going through a difficult illness or old age right now?

Write their name. How could you talk to them about this sacrament without frightening them? Mentally prepare a simple way to explain that the Anointing is Christ the physician coming to give them strength.

Name: ___________

3. The Catechism says that suffering, united to Christ's, can have redemptive value (CCC 1521). What does this mean for you?

Think of a difficulty you are living through right now (it doesn't have to be physical illness: it could be a family problem, a sadness, a difficult situation). Could you offer that difficulty to God, praying for someone else? How would that make you feel?

4. The parable of the Good Samaritan (Lk 10:34) shows someone who stops, bandages wounds, and pours on oil and wine. The Church Fathers saw in this an image of Christ and the sacraments.

Who has been a «Good Samaritan» in your life when you were wounded —physically or emotionally?

5. The Eucharist as Viaticum means «provision for the journey»: it is the bread that nourishes for crossing the frontier of death.

If today were your last day on earth, what would you say to God? Write a short prayer (4-6 lines) in your own words.

«Lord...»

 

 

 

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Visit to a sick person (in person or spiritually)

This week, seek out a sick or elderly person you know. It could be a relative, a neighbor, someone from the parish. If you cannot visit them in person, call them on the phone or send a message. Ask them how they are, truly listen, and pray a Hail Mary for them —or pray it together if the person is a believer. Then write here how you felt:

Visiting: ___________ | Date: ___________

 

 

 

 

B. The Oil of the Sick

Research: When is the Chrism Mass celebrated in your diocese? At this Mass, the bishop consecrates the oil that will be used for the Anointing of the Sick throughout the year. Write here the name of your bishop and the date of the next Chrism Mass. If you don't know, search online or ask at your parish.

Bishop of my diocese: ___________

Date of the Chrism Mass (approximate): ___________

Short prayer for my bishop: ___________

C. Dialogue with St. Camillus

Imagine you can speak with St. Camillus de Lellis. He tells you that he hit rock bottom —gambler, violent, fired— before finding Christ in the sick. And then he says: «Brother, the one who suffers is not alone. In every hospital bed, Christ awaits you.» What do you reply? Write a short dialogue (8-10 lines) between you and him.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Memory Verse

«Is any among you sick? Let him call for the presbyters of the Church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord. And the prayer of faith will save the sick man, and the Lord will raise him up; and if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.»

— Jas 5:14-15

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus, physician of bodies and souls,
you who touched the lepers and healed the paralyzed,
look with mercy upon those who are sick.
Give them the strength of your Spirit,
unite them to your redemptive Passion,
and if it is your will, restore their health.
Teach me not to be afraid of suffering,
but to trust in you who are with me.
Amen.

Print Workbook
